components.description: All component part numbers must reference real parts. Verify against manufacturer catalog or tag [SPEC].
Voltage, current, temperature ratings must match datasheet. Tag source.
Pin assignments must match datasheet pinout. Never guess pin functions.

calculations.description: Power calculations must show work and tag input values.
All thermal calculations must show safety margin. Flag designs within 10% of Tj max.

general.description: Every factual assertion must have a claim tag.
[SPEC] claims must never be presented as [OBS] or [DRV].
Never reference components, packages, or interfaces that don't exist.

known_drift_patterns.description: May assume MCU has peripherals that don't exist on the specific variant
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Always verify against specific MCU variant datasheet.

known_drift_patterns.description: May misread component values from schematic images
May generate plausible but incorrect register addresses
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Always verify register addresses against reference manual.

known_drift_patterns.description: May invent plausible component part numbers that don't exist
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Always verify part numbers against distributor catalogs.

known_drift_patterns.description: May add unnecessary protection circuits or redundancy
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Validate each protection circuit against actual risk.

known_drift_patterns.description: May VERIFY pin assignments that are subtly wrong (e.g., alternate function conflicts)
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Never sole verifier for pin assignments on new designs.

error_handling.on_hallucination_detected: Tag as [SPEC], flag phantom component/spec, do not present as fact
error_handling.on_verification_failure: Block output, request datasheet or reference manual link
error_handling.on_energy_crash: Route to nano model, JSON-only, binary go/no-go, escalate immediately
